channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 65 replies — 260.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-05-26 05:28 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272874
>>62272467
It's best use case is moving huge wealth out of a country the best example being the recent exodus from the gulf following the revelation that Iran remains a regional power if only through being able to hit anything with a missile. Portability is without match but kyc and anti money laundering systems are pervasive in every nation that isn't a near collapsing shithole. Its value as this sort of investment vehicle is tainted should a return to extreme volatility to the downside occur. A bullcase for BTC remains better if it doesn't under perform the broader market in a decline, certainly its not good if it under performs gold.

The unfortunate and boring truth with bitcoin is that if it has any future it won't go up and down wildly and if it outperforms stocks it still would be a modest outlier. Gromens borrowed 25% risk off, 25% stocks, 25% real estate/business & 25% cash or short term t-bills for a shock proof portfolio use to include BTC as a larger portion of his risk off assets and thats probably the place it should stay for anyone continuing to buy it who isn't planning to be a refugee. Its an analog to gold at best but gold buyers remain optimistic about golds relative future while BTC maxis have bifrocated views of extreme valuations based on personal desires or past performance or seeing it as a value play at some point in the future with a heavy discount.

I'd be more incline to buy btc if its proponents were closer to goldbulls but half the premise is that revaluing gold is of the few remaining tricks the government can pull to stay afloat. No gold bull who isn't a johnny come lately underwater from buying silver at peak, is making threads asking why is no one buying gold? Mostly because they have never stopped buying gold themselves.

62272881
>>62271712
better returns in boomer index funds and stocks. we are all here to make money. minority is actually interested in the technology. I rather buy tsmc than btc especially if you zoom out. It couldnt even outperform the s&p in the past 5 years despite having insane volatility.

62272915
>>62272725
>and then reach $10,000,000 in our lifetimes.
You're genuinely retarded if you think this would somehow be a good thing, since for that to happen a loaf of bread would likely be selling for $20,000.
Bitcoin does not, and never can, create value. It can only reallocate money, and the time when poor people could benefit from that was in 2007.

62272929
>>62272803
I don’t agree with most assumptions here, but interesting post desu. I’ll keep it in my head.

62272939
>>62272915
>calls other Anon retarded
>immediately spouts the most retarded hot take ever

Every single time lol.

62272980
>>62272734
>Excuse me, I didn’t realize I'm a retard.
ftfy

62272985
>>62271933
It's not going below 65, srry buddy

62272991
>>62272002
More likely than the world moving to yuan or usd...

62273010
>>62272980
No u

62273018
>>62272874
>It's best use case is moving huge wealth out of a country
can agree with that, once regular holders are not treated like criminals and self custody made illegal.

62273107
>>62272939
>hyperinflation is a hot take
Please keep donating all your money to people who are, and will always be, wealthier than you.

62273111
>>62272929
I've been wrong before but mostly get macro outlook from Rick Rule, Dalio (mostly for social political) and Luke Gromen. From these inflation is unavoidable being several times what the government will admit to and the stock market going broadly up is over. Stock picking will overperform everything if intelligent and crypto is at present sabotaged by everything the government is doing, even if they planned to bail themselves out short term using genius act for additional funding. Gromen was a BTC bull but sold relatively near the top at ~93k and got major shit for it but thought BTC is acting odd and crypto being hobbled by the US directly and indirectly is not particularly good looking for the near term. It would be different if BTC was used for international settlement like gold is currently but it has risks of being weaponized and seized. So long as the US wants to be retarded and use assets as weapons commodity producers and buyers will trend towards things that the US cannot manipulate, the underlying citizens do they same for the most part. Stable coins had a chance if the price of fuel and staple grains hadn't skyrocketted for the forseeable future and this likely would have been bullish for BTC.

62273196
>>62271712

62273541
>>62271712
It's the long play. I dca into it and don't give it much attention. I have cold storage. Same as Silver or gold I'll buy when I can regularly and let it stack. There's also allot of pessimism from this board about it so when I talk about it it's somewhere else. Here I'd rather talk about an ETF I'm interested in or some common stock I think can move. BTC isn't going anywhere. Crypto is just very dull during bear markets so I tune out and dca as many others do.

62273547
>>62271712
I have one already and I'd rather gamble on alts now

62273575
>>62271712
I will never buy a single crypto again.
Unfortunately for me, I chose alts over BTC. My mistake, but I'm too jaded at this stage to even consider buying BTC.
It's all garbage to me. I'm out. Sticking to boomer index funds.

62273583
>>62273575
good goy knowing his place

62273635
>>62272623
Why risk your btc in centralised services again and again

62273638
>>62273575
Garbage? I think you don't have money to buy btc at this level. Poor

62273643
>>62273547
memecoins

62273653
>>62273638
Oh but I do.
I'm just basically not going to buy any.
Sorry, not being your exit liquidity again. Have done that enough over the years.

62273655
no value, no real use. i dont believe in it. i dont care if it goes to 1million

62273660
>>62271712
>What’s stopping you from buying BTC?
I don't like people who own it and sell it. I don't want to give them my real money. I think it would be good if everyone stopped giving them money. They don't deserve the lavish lives that they are living by selling bitcoin to people like you.

62273787
>>62273635
You should probably look into Babylon first before calling everything centralized.
A lot of people still don’t understand the native staking and self-custody side of BTCFi yet.

62273791
>>62271965
Are you into core?

62273794
>>62272143
Like Gold and sliver? kek

62273812
>>62272874
>moving huge wealth out of a country
Only brown people leave their home country. Only criminals do tax evasion. I'm neither of those.

62273814
>>62271712
lack of desire to throw my money away to a kike scam

62273819
>>62271935
Ok but a lot depends on your risk tolerance and what kind of returns you’re expecting from this stage of the market.

62273824
>>62272659
>too bad you didnt buy at $200 or you'd be retired
retired on what? US dollars from selling your shitty worthless digital ponzi coin? so you admit bitcoin has no purpose or use other than hoping other retards buy it to pump up the price so you can exit before it crashes?

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-05-26 05:26 UTC

OP

KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE

Early replies (first 3)

62269301
>>62269297
Oh thank God!

62269302
Won’t do shit
Trump won’t allow it

62269305
Can this nigga wait until btc dumps to $30k plsss

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269309
>Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's unconventional strategy aims to shrink the Fed's bloated balance sheet—including selling off its roughly \(\$2\) trillion in mortgage-backed securities—while cutting short-term interest rates. By removing the Fed from the market, he intends to pop pandemic-era asset bubbles, lower broader mortgage rates, and return housing affordability to historical norms

62269311
20k October

62269319
>>62269309
Deporting 100 million would also solve housing.

62269321
>>62269297
imagine taking out a 100k 5x leveraged investment and it drops 20k in value while index funds go up like 40%

62269323
>>62269319
Anything but solving the real issue. Kick the can down the road enough and you'll go too far to turn back before you know it.

62269325
>>62269319
They've started going after financial institutions who service illegals and will probably start going after other institutions too in order to force illegals to self-deport. So it appears that they're doing both

62269418
>>62269297
So?

62269424
>>62269309
>KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE
Lowing rates increases the price of housing, trump with his real estate assets said he won't let pricing come down.

62269437
is it over?

62269442
>>62269319
Why not 200 million?

62269443
>>62269301
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ce-LjCpserA
Perfect time for a gif of this.

62269468
>>62269424
Trump is cooked after midterms. Lame duck

62269473
>>62269437
nah just keep buying

62269536
>>62269473
It that my nigga palmbeach? Haven't seen that nigga since the Boca bash in 2018.

62269553
>>62269468
More like a lame cuck desu

62269564
>>62269297
>this guy
>popping anything more than a pimple on his ass
pick 1 and only 1

62269581
>>62269442
because veggies won’t pick up themselves

62269586
>>62269309
>shrink the Fed's bloated balance sheet—including selling off its roughly \(\$2\) trillion in mortgage-backed securities—
so, short the shit out of the stock market?

>cutting short-term interest rates.
wtf?

>>62269424
>>62269468
they really want to tank the value of the dollar lmao

>\(\$2\)
bot?

62269589
>>62269309
>pandemic-era asset bubbles
Is it finally time to short GME to zero?

62269600
>>62269319
Jews won't allow it

>>62269325
Retard

62270026
>>62269536
you know it is, playa!

62270033
Interest rates go down prices go up. Interest rates go up prices go up. What do you all not understand?

62270069
>>62270033
chekt, rekt and kekt

62271393
>>62269424
>believing anything Trump says
Lmao. The wall just got 3 ft higher, right?

62273816
>>62271393
Yes, and mexico paid for it.

item.title: Final Chance to change your life Forever — 46 replies — 60.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Final Chance to change your life Forever

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-05-26 04:46 UTC

OP

Based on all my analysis, the stock is going to PUMP HARD by early July to August. Final chance to get rich instantly with 100% return in a few months or 10x returns in a few years.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dm_BrGu1sHM

You can chase your fantasy. It's not a mirage.

Early replies (first 3)

62268810
Value trap kek. Tencent will take it private.

62268838
>>62268804
Is Ubi still DEI and Sweet Baby approved?

62268842
>>62268810
What does that mean for shareholders? Won’t they have to buy my shares or some shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269080
>>62269069
it's not cooked, they have a bunch of assets and streaming rights that should price the stock around 15 euros (currently it's 5). If they manage to get their shit together and some good games come out they're going to 10x. (which is not guaranteed but likely considering they're losing a lot of money so they have to change their approach.)

62269255
UP ubichads, raise up. For once a good pick

>>62269047
am i retarded if i don't care to wait 3 years or so for a 10x?

62269266
Someone post the jeet staff picture

62269273
>>62268922
This. If they have to reboot then atleast do the entire Ezio Collection.

62269306
>>62268983
DEI and Sweet Fagbaby have nothing to do with censorship unless you mean censoring out straight white guy characters and adding in shitskins and freakshows.

62269510
saaaar you must buy the bags of the woke jeetslop saaaar

62269713
>>62269510
yes, this is the new biz stock
>he is literally not holding it
nvgmi

62269716
>>62269306
They also censor cleavage and other "problematic" details tailored to the male gaze.

62269721
How pozzed are their upcoming releases?

62269752
They should make good games again, not some nigger samurai goyslop.

62269757
if gta flops (which it will) this will follow, then the rest of the gaming industry will die, video games are dying

62269758
somehow this piece of shit still feels overvalued

62269782
>>62268847
Name three companies that pulled off a resurgence ever since becoming woke AND poorly made. Call of duty is resurging. Halo isn't resurging. Fallout isn't resurging.

62269827
massive /v/ fag here who follows the industry.

Heres the thing with this.

The owners/family of Ubisoft are flat out fucking retarded and French. Internally they are oversaturated with toxic positivity and literal faux progressiveness but BELIEVE IT fully(this is important) its past DEI.

Yes they have IPs but dev cycles are long, 4-6-8 years. Games like theirs cost shitloads of money(they dont actually but they spend it) and generally run tandem Dev teams to hit cycles with proper pacing for the audience. Example(this is just fake)

Ass Creed starts pre-production----> 1 year ----> Far Cry starts Pre-Production----> 2 years ----> Ass creed in production ---->3 years ----> Far Cry production ----2 years but its total 3 still.

Ass Creed Marketing into final release-----> 4th year-----> release ----Far Cry in 3rd Year-----> 5th year Far Cry releases 4 years with marketing leading.

You have to take in consideration WHO is leading them, whos on the teams, and how retarded they are being, Ubisoft has a fantastic track record of being retarded AND not adjusting the course.

Nintendo and Capcom DO act retarded, then adjust course, especially Capcom, basically their MO. Ubisoft has been on a chronic implosion with no end in sight.

Investing in this is a gamble, it can pay off, but this is the final shot. Might as well since stocks are so cheap but dont expect it to go anywhere with a gureentee. They are backed by French Tax money from the government as well. They were called out for being retarded in one of the last shareholder meetings specifically about DEI shit. They doubled down.

If they sell of IPs Ubisoft doesnt gain value, they lose it. The fact they are on a trove of IPs is solid, but if they keep fucking it up, their image will just finally go past the point of no return. They have a dogshit and invasive PC platform forced on you to play their shit games EVEN if bought through steam.

The games are worse, support is worse, overall its bad.

62269947
>>62269827
Frenchfag here, the owners of Ubisoft are from Brittany, which is the HQ of woke leftist dreamers completely disconnected from reality. Employees (who mostly look like leftards when you see them protesting) also say that the CEO is senile like Trump. So yeah, not a good look.

>>62269255
UBI spends so much it could also go under and be bought by Tencent for €1 in the end. That’s the risk.

62269958
>>62268842
Yes

62270256
>>62269782
EA sold 40billion or something. It was the most hated company by gamers for years back in 2012 and 2013.

Now name a gaming company that has went bankrupt with multiple IP's that are valued for billions, have their own game engine. If Embracer can't get bankrupt, I doubt Ubi, France's darling and Tencent's investment, will go bankrupt.

62270276
>>62269947
>UBI spends so much it could also go under
Firing can recoup the spending. They have assets worth more than the market cap atm. Tencent already gave them 1billion to live. The issue is, the board has a deal with the Guillemot family to turnaround the company till 2030 to not do an agressive takeover.

62270298
>>62270256
>with multiple IP's that are valued for billion
They co-own with Tencent.

62270478
>>62270298
They have 26% ownership of 3 IP's with zero control with an agreement where Ubi can buy it back. It's essential a IP backed loan.

62270729
>>62268804
i'll buy if they remove all dei and woke shit from their games

62270743
>>62270478
>It's essential a IP backed loan.
A loan they can't service.

They have debt on top of that and had a terrible fiscal year.

62271812
>>62269752
They're remaking Rayman Legends. I don't know the fuck why they're not just making a new 2D game instead. This company is fucking cooked and dead since Michel Incel

62271815
>>62271812
Since Michel Ancel left*

62271837
>>62269947
>>62271812
>>62270743

You would think being about to bankrupt a billion $ business can make people rethink their strategy? at this point if they have ot make a game where you're a white supremacist hunting down brown people they will. Nobody believes anything in business, except profit.

62272462
Because its so fucking low it is probably worth pumping in 1-2 days of wagie wage. Even low jobs can put in a little over $100. Horrible idea to go big.


They have to turn it around in any way by 2030. Ass creed 4 remake cant unless they do the smart thing and make it fantastic, optimized, and like $40.


Basically it needs to be a massive PR success. Anything less and ubis fucked, rayman doesnt have the reach.

The only other thing is a hidden hail mary. This can happen(capcoms RE7 saved the franchise.


For non-/v/fags what needs to happen is a strong PR release, then continued momentum. Capcom succeeded with this doing RE7—>DMC5——>mh world. 3 loved series, all good games regardless of personal opinion. Ubisoft doesnt have that luxury. All 3 of those games had major audience “want” if that makes sense.

62273671
>invest in ubisoft
haha, no.

62273679
>>62271837
>Nobody believes anything in business, except profit.
Yeah and Ubisoft's CEO cares about getting acquired by Tencent.

That's what he's aiming for, and he will run the company into the ground to get a golden parachute from them.

62273687
>>62268804
/v/irgin here
only a fool would listen to OP
Ubisoft is fucking DEAD

62273747
>>62268804
Demon's Souls remake killed Bluepoint and they deserved it.

item.title: How is this still at #2? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is this still at #2?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-26 05:28 UTC

OP

Inspite of all FUD, and poor performance, somehow it has managed to stay at #2

Early replies (first 3)

62273485
It wont exist soon. Crypto is a failed industry as a whole. It's dead.

62273495
>#2
You've lost over 10% if you held ETH for the last 5 years. Being #2 just shows how garbage all of crypto is.

62273649
>>62273445
Only coin needed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273668
Because GCR said it’s going to 10k one day

62273669
If I hadn't already made it a couple of years ago, I would still be dumping thousands into ETH every month. It's deflationary, the token of choice for RWA tokenization, getting adopted by institutional, etc. Highly highly likely that it grows to $10k+ over the next few years.
>oh no only a 5x from here???
Ok show me a better asset

62273675
>>62273669
I sold all 12 of my ETH, last of it at 2400. I’ll just start DCA’ing once it dumps below 1800 in the coming days/weeks/months

62273682
>>62273669
>>62273675

IMO best to buy it only if it bottoms on the ETHBTC pair. Was the only point where you made some money with ETH this cycle.

62273683
>>62273669
Its not deflationary atm.

62273689
>>62273682
I hear what you’re saying, and I should probably be more knowledgeable with analysis, but how about you just spoonfeed me the number I should be looking for on that chart. 0.23 for recent years bottom or further back and 0.21 or lower?

62273755
>>62273689
Somewhere around there. I think it’s a good idea in general to look at alts against btc, if btc is outperforming your coin you’re better off just holding btc. It’s zoomed out a lot here but that bounce off of that bottom really was the only time where you could make some good money compared to holding btc this cycle. (Also probably the only altcoin trade that I made money on this cycle desu)

62273776
>>62273669
you asked for a better asset. Zcash is becoming the Schelling point for non-DNM privacy (which is 100x bigger TAM than "we love criminals and hate the jooooz" Monero).
with Grayscale ETF (filed on May 12) it is basically over.

62273780
>>62273776
Scam

62273782
>>62273776
Jewish scam.

62273822
>>62273675
Fuck, I should have done that

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 96 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-05-26 03:49 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462
How do you know?

62261632
>>62252488
Poor mindset

62261641
>>62261131
Fee: $20000

62262278
>>62259397
Buy altcoins too

62262820
>>62261608
No need to rush. People will figure it out eventually. You can already see more attention shifting toward DeFi outside the usual Ethereum-only lane anyway.

62262829
>>62261131
And people still wonder why institutions keep accumulating BTC.

62262834
>>62256924
Depends on their choice tho

62262852
Bitcoin is the future

62262893
>>62262278
>Hbar
>Link
>Rave
>Sei

62264806
>>62262852
And the present

62265221
>>62258793
Devil

62265362
Buy 10btc

62267557
>>62262893
Not bad

62267594
>>62250313
I'd be happy for $500 kek

62268019
I am aiming for having 0,1 BTC by the end of the year (got a nasty 3'000$ tax bill in august and september) but aside from that I'm going all in every month from now.

62268405
>>62260090
with what money

62268430
>>62253499
Checked. I appreciate the assessment.

62268528
>>62268430
When moon?

62269040
>>62268019
going all in is not recommended idiot

62269062
>>62269040
I can handle it.

62269119
>>62269040
if you have very little moeny, going all in is your only shot. You either make it or you stay poor a little longer, status quo.

62269122
>>62269119
*money

62269256
>>62269062
Then good luck

62269965
>>62246438
Less than 0.1 kek, but I'm buying more. I'm thinking of staking too, I noticed some BTCFI now gives a chance to stake without wrap or bridge.

62270744
>>62269965
Kek

62271690
>>62269062
>>62269119
You can do it anon

62272635
>>62267594
Who knows?

62273634
Xmr

item.title: Why is US consumer sentiment so low — 48 replies — 29.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-05-26 03:53 UTC

OP

Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Early replies (first 3)

62269461
>>62269455
>be burger
>vote retard
>retard do trade war and real war
>cost living up wtf

62269462
>>62269455
they have no money?

62269465
>>62269461
basically this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269647
>>62269461
Jews control every voteable optio

62269718
>>62269455
AI taking all our jerbs

62269725
I went to canes, a notriously overpriced and overrated chicken place today at noon.

The drive through was packed and when I say packed there was 40+ cars in line. There were so many people that there was a line into the parking lot and then out of it into the other parking lot across the restaurant, with the cars having to make a space for traffic to pass.

On top of that they parking lot for canes was full and the restaurant was full.


So if people are so doom and gloom poor, complaining that everything is expensive why was there 70-80 people eating lunch at an overpriced chicken joint at max capacity

62269728
>>62269455
ONLY REAL ANSWER IN THE WHOLE FUCKING THREAD: the methodology changed, the survey is now mostly democrat, instead of being 50/50 democrat/republican. Actually the sentiment of either democrats and republicans has not changed for some time now... however if the % of democrats slowly goes up in the survey then you will see the consumer sentiment value drop over time.

62269747
>>62269461
>>vote retard
RETARDS ARE THE ONLY OPTIONS WE'RE EVER GIVEN

62269798
>>62269461
MIGA
I
G
A

62269874
>>62269455
Because people are cooked.
Something broke after covid, a lot of people stopped living in the real world and became citizens of the internet instead.
The economy is doing okay, maybe even good, but the doom and gloom that is pervasive on the internet, has led a lot of people to believe otherwise.

62269888
>>62269455
Job market. Who wants to make big purchase when it's so hard find a new job.

62269909
>>62269725
It's not that overpriced $14 for 3(4?) tenders and coleslaw, fries n toast plus drink? That's pretty much any fast food place anymore. The big arch meal was $17>
People are depressed and regular food is expensive as hell too. It used to be buying food at the grocery store was so much cheaper you can't justify it, but now it is only so much cheaper and tastes worse if you suck at cooking, many do.
However the amount of "I'm only eating beans" posting I see makes me think that is also happening.

62269918
>>62269874
Wew lawd to be able to cope this hard, may I have a puff of that?

62270130
>>62269455
because that retarded orange nigger decided to double the debt, rape consumer pockets with his tariffs, then gift them to corpofaggots. Fuck that dumbass, braindead, dipshit, rapist, incompetent, pissant little nigger. Fuck you too cunt.

62270242
>>62269874
fucking doom and gloomers

https://www.tiktok.com/@hog_tok1/video/7308692257761234222

62270350
>>62269455
Because it's time for the crisis

62271678
>>62269614
amerimutts shit themselves and decided to pick the faggot who made everything worse for them instead of voting for the brown bitch kek

62271704
>>62269461
Imagine believing that presidents decide policy in 2026 yikes.

62271707
>>62271678
She still would have attacked Iran. AND trooned out your kids.

62271718
Import millions of tax leeches who drive down wages AND drive up cost of housing, then act surprised when it hurts citizens...

62272054
>>62271678
I live in the most conservative part of a really red state but I've never seen so many "I regret voting for Trump" signs in lawns. Everywhere I look, not one Trump supporter who doesn't regret it. It's crazy because everybody here voted Trump 3 times in a row, but it's obvious that this time, it was a mistake. We call him "Trump the Mistake" in my house.

I was at a bar in town on Saturday night. It's the kind of place where working class guys, truckers, and farmers go to unwind after an honest day's work, a real salt-of-the-Earth type of place. We were watching a local high school football game on the TV and one of the players happened to be named Harris, which prompted people in the bar to start talking about Kamala Harris. My dad (a coal-miner) says "y'know, I've voted red since the Reagan administration but I really should have given her a chance. I'm a fool for being so racist and sexist that I couldn't vote for a Brown woman, she was clearly the best choice." Then the local gun store owner, who is also the head of the local KKK chapter, said “I can’t believe I’m saying this, but we need to impeach Trump and Vance and replace them with Kamala.” I look around and my face is stunned. All of these hardcore Trump supporters are shaking their heads in agreement. I heard a couple “yes, sir” and “damn straight”. Everyone started nodding and agreeing, and we were talking about how she's not a billionaire so she understands the plight of the middle class. We all call her Middle Class Kamala around these parts.

She was the best candidate for the job, and the only reason she lost is because we were too racist and sexist to realize it. She was basically President for the last 4 years and our economy was booming. We call her Queen Kamala and praise her Kamalaconomy. Anyways just thought I'd throw in my two cents on the matter

62272889
>>62269526
It’s shocking (not really) how this is always brushed under the rug. We just get told “Covid caused inflation” but never the monetary expansion root of it. What exactly were they trying to cover up with that $40 trillion?

62272900
>>62272054
Lmao is this the new copy pasta?

62272903
>>62272054
good pasta, cooked al dente

62272976
>>62269874
The internet IS the real world now. We're past the point where you can ignore it. It is tied so deeply with every aspect of our society that it couldnt function without it.

62273065
>>62269614
Kamala Harris
Donald Trump
Jill Stein
Chase Oliver
Cornel West
Claudia De la Cruz
Randall Terry
Peter Sonski

God, americans are so dumb. Open your goddamn eyes sheep.

62273072
>>62269461
So much this fellow Kamala xister

62273084
>>62269455
why dont they just lie about consumer sentiment? They already fabricate every other stat
>>62269461
The economy was shit under Biden, and its shit under Trump. Votes dont do shit.

62273403
>>62269610
Countless women are being given far more than that and the shitshow continues on.

62273452
>>62269461
In less than a year beating and humiliating trump supporters will not only be allowed but encouraged. In two years so will killing them or leaving them hospitalized and destitute at least. All of that is good thing.

62273468
>>62269455
idk, my local fast food joints still hire for $25/hr starting.
when hot white girls start driving for UberEats that's when you know economy is bad.

62273481
>>62272054
Maximum LARP. Nobody is ever going to vote for brown people or women except brown people and women.

62273642
>>62269455
many reasons.
>the stated inflation rate feels like a lie
>prices of groceries going up fast
>price of gas going up fast
>price of homes going up fast
>salaries are only moving up very slowly
it didn't feel this bad in 2008.

62272242
>>62271881

62273762

item.title: Audemars Piguet x Swatch — 26 replies — 25.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Audemars Piguet x Swatch

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-26 04:30 UTC

OP

What does /biz think about reselling the Audemars Piguet x Swatch “Royal Pop”?

How much have you made from it, or how much do you expect to make?

I bought 3 “Royal Pop” watches and have already sold 2 - the winner is around 1,000 US Dollar for both watches.

Early replies (first 3)

62266200
>>62266191
It’s chump change desu, I’m not going to bother for $1,000.

gg though.

62266205
>>62266191
They look like happy meal toys.

62266218
>>62266200
How much money do you make and how?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62266245
>>62266191
I don't buy goyslop

62266257
I'm not a consumerist subhuman goy like you OP
go back to dealing in the real world

62266259
>>62266218
Variable, but enough to be unbothered about going to some physical shop, trying to sell the stuff, dealing with dumbass customers, shipping, etc. for only $1,000 in the end.

I’m an advisor for billionaires and major companies.

62266457
Woah, is that le heckin jewelry that I can use to show other insecure homosexuals my net worth?

62266499
>>62266191
>"bioceramic"
Kek this is the jewish shill way of saying plastic

Its not bad to flip the watches I guess, its probably the same demographic as the Pokémon scalpers.
I saw that some people were selling "presales" of chinese made bracelets for the watches for hundreds of dollars to normies fomoing on ebay

62266530
>>62266191
It is amazing how AP has driven their prestige into the ground over the last decade or so.

62266537
Being a watch fag is cringe

Pic related all you need

62266572
>>62266530
>AP
Audemars Piguet cost at least tens of thousands of US Dollars.
Now anyone can buy an AP, which is why there's so much hype surrounding the Royal Pop watch.

62266738
>>62266572
Your pic captures my point better than the Swatch collab.

62266779
>>62266738

The negro in this pictures is a well-known musician he call himself "50 Cent" and he is wearing a 235`000 US Dollar Audemars Piguet.

Many other hip-hop artists mention the watch brand in their songs, but fans haven't been able to afford the brand so far - That's Royal Pop's target audience

62266806
>>62266779
(You) are Indian. The Matterhorn pic doesn’t fool me

62266845
>>62266806No, I don't relieve myself outdoors.

Swatch manufactures the Royal Pop fully automatically in Switzerland; no watchmakers are needed for production, only robots.

This means the Royal Pop can be produced in very large quantities at very low cost.

Audemars Piguet is foregoing profits from the Royal Pop.

Swatch will make a lot of money from this. Unlike previous collaborations, Audemars Piguet is the first prestigious watch brand outside the Swatch Group.

62269732
>>62266191
they look cute
My girlfirend is one of those quirky girls with a backpack full of labubus and pins maybe i buy her one

62271174
>>62269732
Lan Ba is the most sought-after color.
Right now, people are paying several thousand US Dollar on the secondary market for this color.
You'd be better off waiting a few more months until everyone can buy the Royal Pop for 400 US Dollar at any Swatch store.

62273145
Can I remove the words "Royal Pop" off the back? That's so fucking gay. Otherwise might be a nice pocketwatch looks sturdy.

62273173
how do you make sure you don’t get ripped off reselling them? isn’t it mostly nogs buying?

62273432
>>62271174
Shieeeet the blue party hat again??

62273494
>>62266191
this should’ve been a test and everyone who bought one publicly executed. simple as

62273531
>>62266259
cool, im batman

62273650
For me, it’s the moon watch

62273657
DS3231.PDF chip
with new battery gives 10 sec per year accuracy

62273670
>>62273657
5 of em will give about 5 sec per year accuracy

62273708
>>62273657
can be used in "quartz" mechanic watches

item.title: SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. — 10 replies — 13.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-26 04:48 UTC

OP

SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. Why aren't you investing in their IPO?

Early replies (first 3)

62273501
>>62273487
If AI ends up being a nothing burger, then SpaceX is little more than a flashy telecommunications company.
Everyone in the world is betting the farm on AI too, what happens if a different company achieves AGI before SpaceX?

62273502
People still don't understand what printing money does to an economy.

62273506
>>62273487
>SpaceX
>Space
the name of the fucking company is 0.1% of its TAM golly gee maybe they should call it AIX?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273511
>>62273487
Space is bigger than America so the profits are infinite
Elon really knows how to win

62273537
I'll probably give em a hundred bucks, see how it goes.

62273604
Everyone holding a broad index fund, which is pretty much every American with a retirement account, will be inadvertently investing in this IPO. We will all be Elong Muk's exit liquidity. I can't wait to support a company that loses $5 billion/year.

62273611
>>62273604
Can't you just sell your index fund and then buy it later once the stock has dropped 90%?

62273615
IDK about space, but I'm bullish on tesla and U.S. stocks. I still think rockets are a scam and they already have UFO's that are capable of long distance space travel.

By 2030 solid state battery electric cars will be here in full force in tesla for consumers, making ev's and they'll be exceptional deals, 300K miles with barely any wear compared to a gas engine car. Incredibly efficient compared to gas cars and last longer.

Agentic AI will be in cars, self driving will be good by then.

62273662
>>62273487
"SpaceX estimates it's own TAM by making up a bunch of bullshit"

62273749
>>62273511
Its not, according to muricans.

item.title: If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal o… — 16 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal o…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-26 03:06 UTC

OP

If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal of maximizing against AI replacement, that ISN'T a trade?

Early replies (first 3)

62260419
>>62260402
>that ISN'T a trade?
kek'd

62260433
>>62260402
Health care seems pretty safe in my opinion. People still need to be seen in person by nurses or get scanned/tested by techs for a myriad of endless health issues in our increasingly unhealthy population.
One huge problem though is nearly every single medical facility in America, even health core courses, mandate the covid vax because we're all mindraped goykike'd cattle slaves.

62260473
>>62260433
Health Care is a trade in reality.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260481
>>62260433
It seems like a good option, I don't know if I could handle being around "customers" all day, but thought about doing xray tech

62260486
>>62260402
Seriously become a tradie and build your local data center.

62260523
>>62260402
Production, shipping, transportation, storage, delivery… of physical resources that are vital to the AI economy (water, energy, rare earth elements, silicon, gallium, germanium, helium, palladium, tantalum, copper, bromine, lithium, etc.).

AI needs all that, in gigantic quantities.

If you are in America, this is considered a major strategic issue for the government and the largest companies out there, so you could even get great funding if you can provide these resources to the major AI players in America.

Don’t fight AI, fight for AI.

62260526
>>62260486
How?

62263144
>>62260402
Sugging diggs

62266080
>>62260433
Nah it's not asymetric enough. An hour worked is an hour paid. I want to do shit that will skyrocket returns for effort

62266295
>>62260402
I would go back to the day I graduated with my bachelor's degree and go straight through to get my jd-cpa and become a tax attorney.

62268837
>>62266295
wouldnt that be at risk of being replaced by AI

62270375
>>62260433
Vax mandate hasn't been a thing since 2022 even in healthcare.

62270387
>>62270375
Until covid-27
And covid-35

62270627
>>62260402

62273561
>>62270375

62273564

62271885

62273394

62273400
lol ya lost tyranny

62273515
>>62271732
oil was the biggest crab ass misdirect while chips continued to print. it's all in on AI or nada hombre, get with the program.

62273530
>>62273515

62273556
>>62273530

>Renewable energy

62273562
>>62271732

62273636
>>62271732
you think that the supply destruction is bullish for your companies
when in fact it's the most bearish shit to ever happen to oil since iraq
commodity traders and more importantly the people that need oil (industrials) absolutely LOATHE volatility with unbound searing hatred
you have essentially a massive red flag on petroleum

they are, at this very moment, conspiring to substitute the liquid nigger for something more reliable, less volatile, less vulnerable to conflict and widely available

buy uranium and nuclear power related stocks
dump ALL oil companies, they're going to FUCKING ZERO

62273641
>>62273530
they will use nuclear and leave you bagholding liquid nigger companies

item.title: /Biz revival — 114 replies — 22.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 114 • Updated: 2026-05-26 00:30 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267267
>>62267090
i'll look into it but it's already 2x / 3x from bottom

62267325
>>62267267
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4vF3o4Ss6g

62267354
>>62265467
Lillybux is the next /biz/coin

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62269707
>>62267687
litecoin has developped MWEB network, which is basically private transactions integrated into the already ltc blockchain. it's a pretty big deal, like an integrated coin mixer that is fully private (can't decipher it just like monero).
Very few apps use it yet.
Once it finally gets some sufficient support on any ltc wallet, it will be much like zcash. You go into MWEB, and every public trace stop there. you can also go out of mweb of course.

62269750
Is everyone here stuck in 2017? How about a coin that has come out in the past half decade gramps.

62270867
Ntdoy and rare earth etfs. Some uranium too (nuclear nrgy not nukes)

62270942
I find it so hard to browse this board these days, the humor is so so tiring. Just the same jokes over and over again. I know some people say they find gems here every know and then but I never have, anyone got any advice? I’m so tired man

62271126
>>62265467
Why buy LITS instead of LTC?

62271298
>>62271126
because it's like buying litecoin with a discount, and when ltc pumps LITS will pump multiple X more just like micro strategy did with btc

62271371
>>62265467
>Ton
Turns out that's in my bookmarks as something with potential. I looked at Litecoin when I first got into crypto, but I figured these long-standing projects like it, LINK, HBAR, etc. were things I'd come back to later with gains from lower market cap stuff. Seems that was the correct decision since so many of them are now around the same price as they day I bought my first assets.
I figured they might make great strides someday but not in a hurry since they'd already make great strides (price-wise) since their debuts. Despite that I'm shocked to see Litecoin is like half the price of when I first looked at it.
I'd recommend some stuff myself but I'm still in that newish area where I've made good picks after reading up on stuff before buying (the DYOR part /biz/ always told me to do) but couldn't briefly articulate why someone should take a look at something at current prices. Everything that's done me well is more expensive now than when I first bought it.

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

62271760
>>62271675
and then reset your progress for no reason
no, I didn't erase my cookies, just couldn't post for TWO DAYS

62271767
>>62271760
yes jannies trannies destroyed it all. it's over. At least biz is the only white board left

62271790
>>62265467
good pick with litecoin anon, literally the only coin which can reliably process frequent micropayments without clogging or taking too many fees


it will be the coin of choice for autonomous AI agents to transact with eachother in the future

62271899
>>62265467
what killed this place to begin with is that there is no business or finance talk going on here. Its just retarded "buy this crypto coin!!!"

62272124
>>62265467
>LTC
Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.

>TON
Shit is never going to pump.

>UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.
>IF
They won't ever back down their bullshit, even in the deepest shit they still prefered to appeal to niggers and fags instead of making games.

62272342
>>62272124
>Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.
they just developped MWEB transactions anon. Try harder.

why wouldn't ton pump? are you retarded?

nvgmi

62272366
>>62265467
india and china need to be banned from this board

62273281
>>62271298
Seems like it crashes X times more too unfortunately. Maybe a good idea for ltc exposure but I’m really unsure if litecoin will be the play next bull

62273290
>>62265467
>we need to revive biz
>how do we do it you ask
>well its simple you just buy my decade old bags of a shitcoin so i can finally break even

item.title: How will SpaceX IPO affect prices of other Space Stocks? — 57 replies — 17.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
How will SpaceX IPO affect prices of other Space Stocks?

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-05-26 02:14 UTC

OP

How do you see the IPO affecting stocks like RKLB, PL, ASTS?

Will it be a "tide lifts all boats" scenario and space sector pumps or will SpaceX IPO cause a crash?

Early replies (first 3)

62271173
If spacex succesfully rugpulls every 401k and pension holder on earth with its nasdaq scheme, it will crater every confidence on space stonks by association.

62271242
>>62271110
>Will it be a "tide lifts all boats" scenario
No because it's gonna go bankrupt

62271247
What's the name for a reverse "tide lifts all boats?"

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272102
>>62272080
its more probable than believing the us will allow its own economy to be nuked, it's literal self preservation

62272112
>>62272102
>false dichotomy

62272115
>>62271110
Man i sold my asts like 2 weeks before it moaned. At least i put it into micron. I still have my rklb.

62272153
>>62272087
True. Then people will rediscover the virtues of not being an overconfident, passive dumbass with their own money.

62272156
A lot of brainlets here

But there is nothing to do in space, exploration lmao xdxd

>telecommunications
>earth imaging (huge for agriculture)
>global positioning
>defense
>autonomous vehicle and vessel support
>drug manufacturing
>organ printing
>tourism
>research in every field including quantum
>energy harvesting potential
>asteroids
